content: We can reframe LoCoMo motion planning latency optimization as a built-in "UNPrompt" mechanism for legged robots, where the latency reduction framework prunes redundant, low-value motion plans the same way UNPrompt techniques prune unneeded tokens from generative model outputs to speed up inference. This allows humanoid loco-manipulation planners to discard implausible motion candidates early in the optimization process, cutting end-to-end latency without sacrificing plan feasibility for dynamic tasks.
